name: Anemone
description: While sometimes considered a threat to coral reefs in high densities, it is a native species and a significant part of the ecosystem. Its presence is notable in the extensive reefs of MIMAROPA.

name: Whale Shark
description: The largest fish in the world, these gentle giants filter-feed on plankton. They are known to aggregate in certain areas of the Philippines, including waters accessible from MIMAROPA.
location: Open waters off Palawan and potentially other parts of MIMAROPA during migration periods..

name: Rabbitfish
description: A popular breakfast fish, often dried, abundant in the shallow waters around Marinduque and Mindoro, associated with seagrass beds and reefs. Multiple species within the genus Siganus are referred to as Danggit.
location: Shallow waters around Marinduque and Mindoro.

name: Dugong
description: A gentle, herbivorous marine mammal that grazes on seagrass beds. Palawan is a significant habitat for this vulnerable species in the Philippines.
location: Seagrass beds around Palawan, as well as parts of Mindoro and Romblon.

name: Green Sea Turtle
description: A large sea turtle species that feeds on seagrass and algae. MIMAROPA hosts important nesting sites and foraging grounds for green sea turtles.
location: Nesting beaches and coastal waters of Palawan, Mindoro (e.g., Sablayan), and other islands in the region.

name: Irrawaddy Dolphin
description: A small, toothed whale found in coastal areas and estuaries. While not strictly marine, they inhabit brackish waters connected to the sea. A population is known to exist in Malampaya Sound, Palawan.

name: Snapper
description: Premium fish with firm white flesh, commonly found in the waters around Palawan and Romblon, associated with reefs and deeper fishing grounds.
location: Coastal waters around Palawan and Romblon.

name: Stingray
description: Graceful filter-feeding rays often seen gliding through the water. They are known to frequent reef areas in Palawan.
location: Reefs and open waters around Palawan.

name: Hawksbill Sea Turtle
description: A critically endangered sea turtle with a distinctive hooked beak, often found in coral reef areas feeding on sponges and other invertebrates.
location: Coral reefs throughout Palawan, Romblon, and other parts of MIMAROPA.

name: Giant Clams)
description: The largest living bivalve mollusk, found in coral reefs. Palawan is known for its populations of giant clams, some of which are part of conservation efforts.
location: Coral reefs of Palawan and other islands in MIMAROPA.

name: Mackerel
description: Commercially important pelagic fish found in the waters of MIMAROPA.
location: Coastal and offshore waters of MIMAROPA.

name: Spanish Mackerel
description: A larger mackerel species favored for its firm texture and rich flavor.

name: Milkfish
description: The milkfish is widely farmed in brackish waters and is a staple in Filipino cuisine.
location: Brackish ponds and coastal waters across Palawan and Mindoro.

name: Tilapia
description: An introduced freshwater fish favored for its adaptability and fast growth.
location: Freshwater lakes and rivers throughout MIMAROPA.

name: Mudfish
description: Mudfish commonly found in freshwater habitats, appreciated for its firm flesh.
location: Swamps, rice paddies, and rivers in Mindoro and Palawan.

name: Catfish
description: Resilient catfish species important in local aquaculture and fishing.

name: Common Carp
description: Common carp introduced for aquaculture, thriving in ponds and lakes.

name: Snakeskin Gourami
description: Snakeskin gourami frequently farmed and found wild in freshwater systems.

name: Silver Perch
description: Endemic silver perch valued for its delicious taste and texture.
location: Freshwater rivers and lakes, especially in Palawan.

name: Small Goby
description: Small goby species common in brackish and freshwater habitats.

name: Giant Freshwater Prawn
description: Giant freshwater prawn prized for size and sweet flavor.

name: Indian Mackerel
description: A type of mackerel rich in oils and flavor.
location: Coastal waters of Palawan and Mindoro.

name: Round Scad
description: Round scad, an affordable and widely consumed fish.

name: Frigate Tuna
description: A small tuna species popular in Filipino dishes.

name: Spanish Mackerel
description: Spanish mackerel known for tender, flavorful meat.

name: Grouper
description: Groupers prized for firm flesh and rich taste.

name: Pompano
description: Known for its tender meat and mild flavor.

name: Yellowfin Tuna
description: Yellowfin tuna prized by commercial fisheries.

name: Squid
description: Squid commonly used in various Filipino recipes.

name: Shrimp
description: Shrimp species valued for sweet meat.

name: Mud Crab
description: Mud crabs famous for sweet meat and often steamed.

name: Blue Swimmer Crab
description: Blue swimmer crabs common in coastal waters.

name: Manila Sea Catfish
description: Endemic marine catfish found in brackish environments.

name: Speckled Goby
description: Small goby found in freshwater and brackish habitats.

name: White Sardine
description: Small pelagic fish often dried or canned.

name: Bloch's Gizzard Shad
description: Common schooling fish near river mouths.

name: Longspine Ponyfish
description: Bioluminescent fish inhabiting shallow waters.

name: Freshwater Sardine
description: The only freshwater sardine endemic to the Philippines.

name: Freshwater Trevally
description: Freshwater trevally known for firm flesh.

name: Picturesque Dragonet
description: Brightly colored reef fish famous for vivid patterns.

name: Arahan Roughback Sea Catfish
description: Sea catfish common in coastal waters and estuaries.
